texto: Dignity is not a symbol bestowed on man, nor does the word itself possess force. Man's dignity is a force and the only modus vivendi by which man and his history survive. When mid-twentieth century Germany did not let man live and die with this right, man became an animal. No matter how technologically advanced or sophisticated, when man negates this divine right, he not only becomes self-destructive, but castrates his history and poisons our future. This is what 'The Nazi Drawings' are about.
explicacao: Citação de Mauricio Lasansky sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Dignity is not a symbol bestowed on man, nor does the word itself possess force.
